title: Hang a Picture Frame
summary: Hang a picture frame on a wall using a nail or hook at the right height.
learnerPromise: You will be able to hang a picture frame level and at a comfortable viewing height using a nail or hook.
whyItMatters: Hanging pictures or artwork personalizes a space and makes it feel like home. Doing it correctly keeps frames secure and walls undamaged.
realLifeUses: Hanging a poster or artwork in a bedroom
Putting up family photos in a living room
Mounting a whiteboard or corkboard in a study area
Decorating a dorm room or new apartment
materialsNeeded: Level or a phone with a level app
result: A securely hung picture frame that is level and at a comfortable viewing height.
youWillLearn: How to choose the right height for hanging a frame
How to find the hanging point on the back of the frame
How to mark and place a nail or hook in the wall
How to check that the frame is level before finishing
miniChallenge: Hang one picture frame, poster, or lightweight item on a wall today. Make sure it is level.
steps: Decide on placement and measure the center height, about 57 to 60 inches from the floor for artwork.
Check the back of the frame for a wire, hook, or bracket to find where it will hang from.
Mark the wall lightly with a pencil where the nail or hook needs to go.
Hammer the nail or insert the hook at a slight upward angle, leaving enough exposed to catch the frame.
Hang the frame, step back, and check if it is level. Adjust if needed.
completionCriteria: Frame is securely attached and does not wobble when lightly touched
Frame appears level when viewed from a few feet away
commonProblems: Frame tilts to one side — the hanging wire may be off-center, adjust or use two nails
Nail bends while hammering — switch to a sturdier nail or a thinner wall hook
Not sure if it is level — use a level app on your phone placed on top of the frame
tips: For heavier frames, use a wall anchor or stud finder for extra support
Hold the frame up to the wall before committing to check if you like the placement
Use painter's tape to mark corners lightly before making holes
